name: step-by-step
description: /step-by-step (alias /sbs) — plan-first sequential runbook with TWO modes (auto · manual default · 0.5.0 2-mode redesign). MANUAL (the DEFAULT) — chat-form disambiguation (1 question per round, easy-mode 7-element scaffold: icon · canonical name · friendly alias · plain-line · everyday analogy · ASCII sketch · option compare-table · one-line recommendation; closed with `→ A · B · 또는 자유응답` so free-form answers are explicitly OK) round-by-round until a scan finds ZERO remaining ambiguity, THEN a `🎯 합의된 결정셋 (N개)` ASCII tree of every accumulated decision PAUSE — the user types `go` to confirm or `Qn=<other>` to flip ONLY that decision (re-render after each flip). On `go`, write `drafts/<slug>-plan.md` (gitignored · frontmatter slug/mode/auto-weights/created · task brief · locked decisions · next-action checklist ending with a ship line · completion criteria) and LAUNCH A BACKGROUND AGENT (general-purpose, run_in_background=true) with a self-contained prompt containing the plan.md + ship instructions + completion criteria — the agent runs end-to-end (including ship) with no further user input; user can leave. AUTO — same chat-form scaffold output, but each question is AUTO-PICKED by the 4-axis weighted average instead of waiting for the user: 완성도 (complete · robust + edge-case coverage) + 단순 (simple · Occam's razor) + 안전 (safe · blast radius minimized) + 표준 (std · fits the sidecar pattern), default 1:1:1:1, inline override `/sbs auto:safety <task>` (single-axis force) or `/sbs auto:complete=2,simple=3 <task>` (explicit weights). AUTO still pauses at the agreement screen — auto-picks get a user pre-commit checkpoint. Mid-run new ambiguity also opens a chat round + re-renders the agreement screen. Mode = FIRST arg token `auto`[:`<axis-or-weights>`] | `manual` (default manual). `legacy-manual` = the old per-step pause behavior — 1-version deprecation banner, use plain `manual` going forward. `go` is NOT a mode (use `/go` to continue the paused flow). ALL modes halt on a step failure (failing step + verbatim error + un-run tail) and pause before a genuinely irreversible / destructive / outward-facing step (confirm-then-resume, same bar as the bypass self-check). The deliberate single-threaded counterpart to /cycle's parallel fan-out — chat-disambiguate → lock → plan.md → background handoff.
